states.title: Baseline EKG due at your next Class 1 exam
states.body: Under 14 CFR 67.111(b)(1), a person applying for a First-Class medical must show an EKG at their first application after reaching age 35. We don't have one logged for you yet. The EKG must be dated within 60 days of the exam it accompanies (14 CFR 67.111(c)).

states.title: Baseline EKG done — no annual EKG until age 40
states.body: Your baseline EKG is on file. Under 14 CFR 67.111(b)(1), this one-time baseline satisfies the requirement for First-Class applications between ages 35 and 39. The annual cadence under 14 CFR 67.111(b)(2) starts on your 40th birthday.

states.title: Your next Class 1 exam will require a fresh EKG
states.body: Under 14 CFR 67.111(b)(2), every First-Class medical application at age 40 or older requires an EKG. Under 14 CFR 67.111(c), each EKG must be dated within 60 days of the exam it accompanies, so a previous EKG does not carry forward to a future exam. Plan to have a new EKG done within 60 days of your next Class 1 application.
